title: oxalis medicaginea
intro: Oxalis medicaginea, also known as creeping woodsorrel, is a perennial plant with heart-shaped leaves and yellow flowers that grow in clusters. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues. Oxalis medicaginea can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is often used in herbal remedies for its purported health benefits.
